commands.review_summary:
The PR updates browser attachment readiness matching for generated `attachments-bundle.txt` and `.zip` uploads, adds FakeDocument regression tests, and records the fix in the unreleased changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and the added fixture, but not from a live browser run. Current main has no explicit stem-only generated bundle match, while the branch adds that path for `attachments-bundle.txt` and `.zip`.

target.title: Relax generated bundle attachment readiness
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a browser-mode attachment readiness edge case for Oracle-generated browser bundles.

ChatGPT can expose bundled uploads as a visible chip with only `attachments-bundle` or a duplicate-renamed label like `attachments-bundle(13).txt`. The pre-send gate should accept that for Oracle's generated `attachments-bundle.txt` / `.zip` files, but should not relax matching for arbitrary user files.

## Changes

- Marks Oracle-generated `attachments-bundle.txt` / `.zip` expectations explicitly.
- Adds a stem-only fallback only for those generated bundle files.
- Tightens filename boundary checks so embedded names like `not-attachments-bundle.txt` do not satisfy the gate.
- Adds regression coverage for bundle stem matching, duplicate-renamed bundle chips, non-bundle stem rejection, and embedded-name rejection.
- Adds a changelog entry under `0.13.1 — Unreleased`.

## Validation

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm vitest run tests/browser/promptComposerExpressions.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run tests/browser/promptComposer.test.ts tests/browser/attachmentsCompletion.test.ts`
- `pnpm run build`
- `pnpm run format:check`
